content.text: that accepts a string query and returns a list of
(source, page number, URL, etc.). Retrievers are the backbone of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) — they fetch the relevant chunks that the LLM uses to ground its answer.

attrs.title: A query enters a Retriever; Documents are returned and injected into the LLM prompt

content.text: The most common retriever wraps a vector store. Each document is embedded into a high-dimensional vector; at query time the query is embedded and the

from langchain_community.vectorstores import Chroma
from langchain_anthropic import AnthropicEmbeddings
from langchain_core.documents import Document

# Build a vector store from documents
docs = [
    Document(page_content="LangChain is a framework for LLM apps.", metadata={"source": "intro.txt"}),
    Document(page_content="LCEL uses the pipe operator to compose chains.", metadata={"source": "lcel.txt"}),
    Document(page_content="Retrievers return a list of Document objects.", metadata={"source": "retriever.txt"}),
]

embeddings = AnthropicEmbeddings(model="voyage-3")   # or OpenAIEmbeddings(), etc.
vectorstore = Chroma.from_documents(docs, embeddings)

# Convert to a retriever — this is a Runnable[str, list[Document]]
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever(
    search_type="similarity",   # or "mmr" for diversity
    search_kwargs={"k": 3},     # return top 3 chunks
)

results = retriever.invoke("What is LCEL?")
for doc in results:
    print(doc.page_content)
    print(doc.metadata)

# MMR (Maximal Marginal Relevance) — balances relevance and diversity
mmr_retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever(
    search_type="mmr",
    search_kwargs={"k": 4, "fetch_k": 20, "lambda_mult": 0.5},
)
FAISS (Facebook AI Similarity Search) is the standard local vector store — no server required, fast for datasets up to millions of documents, and easily persisted to disk.

from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_text_splitters import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter

# Split long documents into overlapping chunks
splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(
    chunk_size=500,
    chunk_overlap=50,
    separators=["\n\n", "\n", " ", ""],
)
raw_text = """LangChain is an open-source framework.
It provides tools for building LLM applications.
The key abstraction is the Runnable interface."""

chunks = splitter.create_documents([raw_text])

# Build FAISS index in memory
faiss_store = FAISS.from_documents(chunks, embeddings)

# Persist to disk and reload
faiss_store.save_local("faiss_index")
faiss_store = FAISS.load_local("faiss_index", embeddings, allow_dangerous_deserialization=True)

retriever = faiss_store.as_retriever(search_kwargs={"k": 2})

# Similarity search with score threshold
scored_retriever = faiss_store.as_retriever(
    search_type="similarity_score_threshold",
    search_kwargs={"score_threshold": 0.7},
)
A single query may miss relevant chunks due to vocabulary mismatch.
uses an LLM to rewrite the original query into several variants, retrieves documents for each, and returns the union — improving recall without manual query engineering.

from langchain.retrievers import MultiQueryRetriever
from langchain_anthropic import ChatAnthropic

llm = ChatAnthropic(model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001")
base_retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever(search_kwargs={"k": 3})

multi_retriever = MultiQueryRetriever.from_llm(
    retriever=base_retriever,
    llm=llm,
)

# For the query "how do I build a chain?" the LLM might generate:
# 1. "what is the process for creating a LangChain chain?"
# 2. "LangChain chain construction tutorial"
# 3. "pipe operator chain example LangChain"
# Results from all three are deduplicated and returned.

docs = multi_retriever.invoke("how do I build a chain?")
print(f"Retrieved {len(docs)} unique documents")
Vector search returns full chunks regardless of how much of each chunk is relevant.
that either extracts the relevant sentences (
) or filters out irrelevant chunks entirely (

from langchain.retrievers import ContextualCompressionRetriever
from langchain.retrievers.document_compressors import LLMChainExtractor, LLMChainFilter

base_retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever(search_kwargs={"k": 5})

# Extract only the relevant sentences from each chunk
extractor = LLMChainExtractor.from_llm(llm)
compression_retriever = ContextualCompressionRetriever(
    base_compressor=extractor,
    base_retriever=base_retriever,
)

docs = compression_retriever.invoke("What does LCEL stand for?")
# Each doc.page_content contains only the relevant excerpt

# Filter out chunks that aren't relevant at all
filter_compressor = LLMChainFilter.from_llm(llm)
filter_retriever = ContextualCompressionRetriever(
    base_compressor=filter_compressor,
    base_retriever=base_retriever,
)

# EmbeddingsFilter — faster, no LLM call for compression
from langchain.retrievers.document_compressors import EmbeddingsFilter
embed_filter = EmbeddingsFilter(embeddings=embeddings, similarity_threshold=0.76)
fast_compression = ContextualCompressionRetriever(
    base_compressor=embed_filter,
    base_retriever=base_retriever,
)

BM25 (Best Match 25) is the standard TF-IDF-based ranking algorithm used by search engines. It is fast, needs no embeddings model, and outperforms vector search for exact keyword matches and rare terms. Hybrid search combines BM25 and vector search for the best of both worlds.

from langchain_community.retrievers import BM25Retriever

# Build a BM25 retriever directly from documents (no embedding)
bm25 = BM25Retriever.from_documents(docs)
bm25.k = 3   # top-3 results

results = bm25.invoke("LangChain framework")

# Hybrid retrieval — combine BM25 and vector scores
from langchain.retrievers import EnsembleRetriever

vector_ret = vectorstore.as_retriever(search_kwargs={"k": 3})
bm25_ret   = BM25Retriever.from_documents(docs, k=3)

hybrid = EnsembleRetriever(
    retrievers=[vector_ret, bm25_ret],
    weights=[0.6, 0.4],   # 60% vector, 40% BM25
)
results = hybrid.invoke("LangChain pipe operator")
Wiring a Retriever into a RAG Chain
fetches the context while passing the original question through, so both are available in the prompt template.

from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate
from langchain_core.runnables import RunnablePassthrough
from langchain_core.output_parsers import StrOutputParser
from langchain_anthropic import ChatAnthropic

def format_docs(docs: list) -> str:
    return "\n\n".join(d.page_content for d in docs)

rag_prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system",
     "Answer the question using only the context provided. "
     "If the answer is not in the context, say 'I don't know.'\n\n"
     "Context:\n{context}"),
    ("human", "{question}"),
])

llm = ChatAnthropic(model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001")

rag_chain = (
    RunnablePassthrough.assign(context=retriever | format_docs)
    | rag_prompt
    | llm
    | StrOutputParser()
)

answer = rag_chain.invoke({"question": "What is LCEL?"})
print(answer)

# Streaming
for chunk in rag_chain.stream({"question": "What is LCEL?"}):
    print(chunk, end="", flush=True)
Any data source can become a retriever by subclassing
. This is useful for SQL databases, REST APIs, proprietary search indexes, or any non-vector retrieval strategy.

from langchain_core.retrievers import BaseRetriever
from langchain_core.documents import Document
from langchain_core.callbacks import CallbackManagerForRetrieverRun

class SQLRetriever(BaseRetriever):
    """Retrieves documents from a SQLite full-text search table."""
    db_path: str
    table: str
    k: int = 5

    def _get_relevant_documents(
        self,
        query: str,
        *,
        run_manager: CallbackManagerForRetrieverRun,
    ) -> list[Document]:
        import sqlite3
        conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)
        rows = conn.execute(
            f"SELECT content, source FROM {self.table} "
            f"WHERE content MATCH ? LIMIT ?",
            (query, self.k),
        ).fetchall()
        return [
            Document(page_content=row[0], metadata={"source": row[1]})
            for row in rows
        ]

sql_retriever = SQLRetriever(db_path="knowledge.db", table="docs_fts", k=3)
results = sql_retriever.invoke("retriever tutorial")
Splitting documents into small chunks improves retrieval precision but loses surrounding context.
indexes small child chunks for search but returns the larger parent chunk when a child matches — giving the LLM full context around the matched passage.

from langchain.retrievers import ParentDocumentRetriever
from langchain.storage import InMemoryStore
from langchain_text_splitters import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter
from langchain_community.vectorstores import Chroma

# Parent splitter — larger chunks stored as context
parent_splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(chunk_size=2000)
# Child splitter — smaller chunks indexed for retrieval
child_splitter  = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(chunk_size=400)

# The docstore holds parent documents keyed by ID
docstore    = InMemoryStore()
vectorstore = Chroma(embedding_function=embeddings)

retriever = ParentDocumentRetriever(
    vectorstore=vectorstore,
    docstore=docstore,
    child_splitter=child_splitter,
    parent_splitter=parent_splitter,
)

retriever.add_documents(docs)

# At query time: search child chunks, return parent chunks
results = retriever.invoke("What is LCEL?")
for doc in results:
    print(f"Parent chunk ({len(doc.page_content)} chars): {doc.page_content[:120]}...")
# Each result is a full 2000-char parent chunk, not a 400-char snippet
When documents have structured metadata (year, category, author),
uses an LLM to translate the user's natural-language query into both a semantic search query and a metadata filter, applied simultaneously.

from langchain.retrievers.self_query.base import SelfQueryRetriever
from langchain.chains.query_constructor.base import AttributeInfo

# Describe the metadata fields in the vector store
metadata_field_info = [
    AttributeInfo(name="source", description="The filename of the document", type="string"),
    AttributeInfo(name="year",   description="The publication year",         type="integer"),
    AttributeInfo(name="topic",  description="The topic category",           type="string"),
]

self_query_retriever = SelfQueryRetriever.from_llm(
    llm=llm,
    vectorstore=vectorstore,
    document_contents="Technical documentation for LangChain",
    metadata_field_info=metadata_field_info,
    enable_limit=True,   # allow "give me 2 documents" style queries
)

# The LLM decomposes this into:
#   semantic query: "LCEL pipe operator"
#   metadata filter: year == 2024 AND topic == "frameworks"
results = self_query_retriever.invoke(
    "Show me 2024 documents about LCEL from the frameworks category"
)
Two standard metrics measure retrieval quality without running the full RAG pipeline:
— the fraction of queries where at least one relevant document appears in the top-k results.
— the mean of 1/rank for the first relevant document; rewards putting the right chunk higher.
— they slot directly into LCEL chains via
FAISS and Chroma are the standard local vector stores; convert any store to a retriever via
improves recall by generating query variants;
improves precision by extracting only relevant sentences.
fuses BM25 keyword scores with vector similarity scores for hybrid retrieval.
to wrap any custom data source as a retriever.
